source.notes: Numeric identifiers and canonical names follow the BACnet engineering-units enumeration. Reserved/proprietary range markers are intentionally omitted; only standardized metrics are listed.

metrics.name: square-meters
metrics.notes: SI base for area; equals 10.7639 ft².

metrics.name: milliamperes
metrics.notes: 1/1000 of an ampere; commonly used for 4–20 mA process loops.

metrics.name: amperes
metrics.notes: SI base unit of electric current.

metrics.name: volts
metrics.notes: SI unit of electric potential; J/C.

metrics.name: kilovolts
metrics.notes: 1 kV = 1 000 V; used for medium-voltage distribution.

metrics.name: volt-amperes
metrics.notes: Apparent power S = V·I (RMS); equals real power only at unity PF.

metrics.name: kilovolt-amperes
metrics.notes: 1 kVA = 1 000 VA; common transformer rating unit.

metrics.name: megavolt-amperes
metrics.notes: 1 MVA = 1 000 000 VA; large transformer / substation rating.

metrics.name: volt-amperes-reactive
metrics.notes: Reactive power Q = V·I·sin(φ); positive for inductive loads.

metrics.name: degrees-phase
metrics.notes: Phase angle between voltage and current; typically –90°…+90°.

metrics.name: power-factor
metrics.description: Ratio of real to apparent power (-1..1)
metrics.notes: Dimensionless ratio P/S in –1…+1; sign indicates leading/lagging.

metrics.name: joules
metrics.notes: SI unit of energy; 1 J = 1 W·s.

metrics.name: kilowatt-hours
metrics.notes: 1 kWh = 3.6 MJ; standard utility-billing unit for active energy.

metrics.name: therms
metrics.notes: 1 thm ≈ 105.5 MJ (≈ 100 000 BTU); US natural-gas billing.

metrics.name: ton-hours
metrics.notes: 1 ton-hour ≈ 12 660 BTU = 3.517 kWh of cooling energy.

metrics.name: joules-per-kilogram-dry-air
metrics.notes: Specific enthalpy referenced to dry-air mass for psychrometrics.

metrics.name: hertz
metrics.notes: SI unit of frequency; 1 Hz = 1 cycle/s.

metrics.name: grams-of-water-per-kilogram-dry-air
metrics.display_name: Grams Of Water Per Kilogram Dry Air
metrics.notes: Absolute humidity ratio: mass of water vapour per kg of dry air.

metrics.name: watts-per-square-foot
metrics.notes: Solar/thermal irradiance in imperial units; 1 W/ft² ≈ 10.764 W/m².

metrics.name: lumens
metrics.notes: Total luminous flux emitted by a source.

metrics.name: luxes
metrics.notes: Illuminance at a surface; 1 lx = 1 lm/m².

metrics.name: tons
metrics.notes: Short ton = 2 000 lb ≈ 907.18474 kg (not the metric tonne).

metrics.name: watts
metrics.notes: SI unit of power; 1 W = 1 J/s.

metrics.name: pascals
metrics.notes: SI unit of pressure; 1 Pa = 1 N/m². Often too small for HVAC ranges.

metrics.name: centimeters-of-water
metrics.notes: Pressure as height of a water column; 1 cmH₂O ≈ 98.0665 Pa (4 °C).

metrics.name: inches-of-water
metrics.notes: 1 inH₂O ≈ 248.84 Pa (60 °F); common for low HVAC duct pressure.

metrics.name: inches-of-mercury
metrics.notes: 1 inHg ≈ 3 386.39 Pa; barometric-pressure convention in the US.

metrics.name: degrees-kelvin
metrics.notes: BACnet name uses 'degrees-kelvin' but kelvin is dimensionless ('K', not '°K').

metrics.name: degree-days-celsius
metrics.notes: Heating/cooling degree-days summed over the period (°C·day).

metrics.name: degree-days-fahrenheit
metrics.notes: Heating/cooling degree-days summed over the period (°F·day).

metrics.name: years
metrics.notes: Calendar year; precise length depends on definition (use with care).

metrics.name: feet-per-minute
metrics.notes: 1 ft/min = 0.00508 m/s; common for air-velocity sensors.

metrics.name: cubic-meters
metrics.notes: SI unit of volume; 1 m³ = 1 000 L.

metrics.name: imperial-gallons-per-minute
metrics.notes: Imperial gallons per minute (≠ US GPM).

metrics.name: no-units
metrics.notes: Use when no engineering unit applies (counters, indices, raw counts).

metrics.name: parts-per-million
metrics.notes: 1 ppm = 1e-6 mass/volume fraction depending on context.

metrics.name: percent-per-second
metrics.notes: Rate of change of a percent value (e.g., damper position derivative).

metrics.name: currency1
metrics.notes: BACnet currency slot 1; meaning is locally configured by the site (e.g., USD, EUR).

metrics.name: currency2
metrics.notes: BACnet currency slot 2; meaning is locally configured by the site.

metrics.name: currency3
metrics.notes: BACnet currency slot 3; meaning is locally configured by the site.

metrics.name: currency4
metrics.notes: BACnet currency slot 4; meaning is locally configured by the site.

metrics.name: currency5
metrics.notes: BACnet currency slot 5; meaning is locally configured by the site.

metrics.name: currency6
metrics.notes: BACnet currency slot 6; meaning is locally configured by the site.

metrics.name: currency7
metrics.notes: BACnet currency slot 7; meaning is locally configured by the site.

metrics.name: currency8
metrics.notes: BACnet currency slot 8; meaning is locally configured by the site.

metrics.name: currency9
metrics.notes: BACnet currency slot 9; meaning is locally configured by the site.

metrics.name: currency10
metrics.notes: BACnet currency slot 10; meaning is locally configured by the site.

metrics.name: square-inches
metrics.notes: Imperial area unit; 1 in² = 6.4516 cm² = 0.00064516 m².

metrics.name: square-centimeters
metrics.notes: Metric subdivision of m²; 1 m² = 10 000 cm².

metrics.name: btus-per-pound
metrics.notes: Specific enthalpy of a fluid (per unit mass).

metrics.name: millivolts
metrics.notes: 1 mV = 0.001 V; thermocouple and sensor signal range.

metrics.name: kilojoules-per-kilogram
metrics.notes: Specific energy / specific heat content (per unit mass).

metrics.name: joules-per-degree-kelvin
metrics.notes: Heat capacity; 1 J/K is small (typical building values are MJ/K).

metrics.name: hectopascals
metrics.notes: 1 hPa = 100 Pa = 1 mbar; standard barometric-pressure unit.

metrics.name: kw-hours-per-square-meter
metrics.notes: Energy use intensity; cumulative energy normalized by floor area.

metrics.name: megajoules-per-square-meter
metrics.notes: Energy use intensity (SI energy, SI floor area).

metrics.name: megajoules-per-square-foot
metrics.notes: Energy use intensity (SI energy, imperial floor area).

metrics.name: watts-per-square-meter-degree-kelvin
metrics.display_name: Watts Per Square Meter Degree Kelvin

metrics.name: percent-obscuration-per-foot
metrics.notes: Smoke obscuration per foot of beam path; smoke-detector sensitivity.

metrics.name: percent-obscuration-per-meter
metrics.notes: Smoke obscuration per metre of beam path; smoke-detector sensitivity.

metrics.name: milliohms
metrics.notes: 1 mΩ = 0.001 Ω; shunt-resistor and contact-resistance range.

metrics.name: mega-btus
metrics.notes: 1 MBTU = 1 000 000 BTU = 1 000 kBTU (≠ MMBTU in US gas industry).

metrics.name: newton
metrics.notes: SI unit of force; 1 N = 1 kg·m/s².

metrics.name: tons-per-hour
metrics.notes: Metric tonnes per hour (1 t = 1 000 kg).

metrics.name: millimeters-per-second
metrics.notes: 1 mm/s = 0.001 m/s; used for vibration severity.

metrics.name: amperes-per-meter
metrics.notes: Magnetic field strength H; Ampère-turns per meter.

metrics.name: farads
metrics.notes: SI unit of capacitance; 1 F = 1 C/V (typically µF or nF in practice).

metrics.name: henrys
metrics.notes: SI unit of inductance; 1 H = 1 V·s/A.

metrics.name: siemens
metrics.notes: SI unit of conductance, the reciprocal of the ohm (1 S = 1/Ω).

metrics.name: siemens-per-meter
metrics.notes: Bulk electrical conductivity; reciprocal of resistivity.

metrics.name: teslas
metrics.notes: SI unit of magnetic flux density; 1 T = 1 Wb/m² = 10 000 G.

metrics.name: webers
metrics.notes: SI unit of magnetic flux; 1 Wb = 1 V·s.

metrics.name: candelas
metrics.notes: SI base unit of luminous intensity.

metrics.name: candelas-per-square-meter
metrics.notes: Luminance; perceived brightness of a surface.

metrics.name: degrees-kelvin-per-minute
metrics.notes: Rate of temperature change (1 K/min = 60 K/h).

metrics.name: watts-per-meter-per-degree-kelvin
metrics.display_name: Watts Per Meter Per Degree Kelvin

metrics.name: microsiemens
metrics.notes: 1 µS = 1e-6 S; typical range for ultrapure water conductivity.

metrics.name: decibels
metrics.notes: Logarithmic ratio: 10·log₁₀(P₁/P₂) for power, 20·log₁₀ for amplitude.

metrics.name: decibels-millivolt
metrics.notes: Voltage level relative to 1 mV: 20·log₁₀(V/1 mV).

metrics.name: decibels-volt
metrics.notes: Voltage level relative to 1 V: 20·log₁₀(V/1 V).

metrics.name: millisiemens
metrics.notes: 1 mS = 0.001 S; common range for water/solution conductivity.

metrics.name: grams-per-gram
metrics.notes: Dimensionless mass ratio; equivalent to a pure number.

metrics.name: kilograms-per-kilogram
metrics.notes: Dimensionless mass ratio; equivalent to a pure number.

metrics.name: milligrams-per-liter
metrics.notes: 1 mg/L ≈ 1 ppm in dilute aqueous solutions.

metrics.name: micrograms-per-liter
metrics.notes: 1 µg/L ≈ 1 ppb in dilute aqueous solutions.

metrics.name: becquerels
metrics.notes: SI unit of activity: 1 Bq = 1 decay/s.

metrics.name: sieverts
metrics.notes: Equivalent dose; 1 Sv = 1 J/kg weighted by radiation type.

metrics.name: decibels-a
metrics.notes: A-weighting approximates human ear response (≈ 1 kHz centred).

metrics.name: nephelometric-turbidity-unit
metrics.notes: Optical turbidity; calibration matters for cross-instrument comparability.

metrics.name: minutes-per-degree-kelvin
metrics.notes: Inverse of K/min; useful for time-constant style metrics.

metrics.name: volt-square-hours
metrics.notes: V²h: integral of voltage squared over time; used in power-quality metering.

metrics.name: ampere-square-hours
metrics.notes: A²h: integral of current squared over time; used in I²t/thermal modelling.

metrics.name: joule-per-hours
metrics.notes: Energy delivery rate expressed in J/h (1 J/h ≈ 0.2778 mW).

metrics.name: watt-hours-per-cubic-meter
metrics.notes: Energy density per volume of fluid (e.g., specific energy of fuel).

metrics.name: joules-per-cubic-meter
metrics.notes: Energy density per volume; 1 Wh/m³ = 3 600 J/m³.

metrics.name: mole-percent
metrics.notes: Mole fraction expressed as a percent.

metrics.name: million-standard-cubic-feet-per-minute
metrics.display_name: Million Standard Cubic Feet Per Minute
metrics.notes: Standard conditions per BACnet; convert to actual conditions before integrating.

metrics.name: standard-cubic-feet-per-day
metrics.notes: SCFD: gas flow at standard reference conditions; not actual flow.

metrics.name: million-standard-cubic-feet-per-day
metrics.display_name: Million Standard Cubic Feet Per Day
metrics.notes: Million SCFD; report at the standard reference conditions defined by the source.

metrics.name: thousand-cubic-feet-per-day
metrics.notes: Thousand cubic feet per day at flowing conditions.

metrics.name: thousand-standard-cubic-feet-per-day
metrics.display_name: Thousand Standard Cubic Feet Per Day

metrics.name: pounds-mass-per-day
metrics.notes: Imperial mass flow per day; 1 lb/d ≈ 0.000005 kg/s.
